name: Westlaw Edge API
what_it_does: Thomson Reuters' enterprise REST API providing programmatic access to the most comprehensive US legal database including case law, statutes, regulations, secondary sources, and AI-powered legal analytics.
use_cases: Enterprise law firm applications requiring comprehensive US case law and secondary sources
Legal analytics platforms performing large-scale precedent and citation analysis
Automated contract review tools needing authoritative statutory and regulatory references
In-house legal department tools integrating case law into workflow applications
Legal research assistants requiring KeyCite citation validation and treatment history
not_for: Startups or small firms without existing Westlaw subscriptions ($2000+/month)
Public-interest or nonprofit projects that cannot afford enterprise licensing
Quick prototyping or API evaluation without going through Thomson Reuters sales
International law research outside Thomson Reuters coverage areas
best_when: You are an enterprise law firm or legal tech company with an existing Westlaw subscription that needs to embed authoritative US legal research into custom applications.
avoid_when: Budget is constrained, you need rapid API access without a sales process, or your use case doesn't justify Westlaw subscription costs.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow for enterprise integrations; API key also available; authentication tied to organizational Westlaw subscription; approval process required

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise subscription required; pricing typically $2000-$5000+/month depending on seat count and data access; API access negotiated separately
pricing.notes: No self-serve pricing. Must contact Thomson Reuters sales. API access typically bundled with enterprise Westlaw subscription. Volume pricing available for large firms.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by subscription tier; enterprise tiers typically allow high request volumes

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses are functional but documentation on error codes is limited for external developers; enterprise support compensates
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Primarily read-only research queries; safe to retry
known_agent_gotchas: API access requires existing enterprise Westlaw subscription — cannot trial independently
Sales and approval process can take weeks before API credentials are issued
Documentation is not publicly accessible without a developer account
Rate limits and quotas are not published; must negotiate with account manager
KeyCite citation flags require careful parsing and legal expertise to interpret correctly in agent context
Terms of service restrict AI/ML model training on retrieved content

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise-grade security appropriate for sensitive legal data; SOC2 and ISO27001 certified; strong auth but complexity creates integration friction
